markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Behold My Creation!](/Feats.aspx?ID=7478) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 95

**Archetype** [Lepidstadt Surgeon](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=302)

**Prerequisites**
[Let my Creature Live!](/Feats.aspx?ID=7476)

</column>

---

You have rebuilt your creation once more. It is greater now, more than it once was. Soon, it shall be greater still. Your construct companion becomes an [incredible construct companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=1613).

<title level="2">Behold My Creation! leads to...</title>
[A Miracle of Science!](/Feats.aspx?ID=7481)
name: Behold My Creation!

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stasian Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=7479) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 95

**Archetype** [Lepidstadt Surgeon](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=302)

**Prerequisites**
[In Lightning, Life](/Feats.aspx?ID=7473)

</column>

---

Charging a living body with electricity may seem like a bad idea, but you know better. When a creature gains temporary Hit Points from your In Lightning, Life, they gain the [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) condition until the end of their next turn. They can use the extra action only for Stride and Strike actions.
name: Stasian Charge

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stand Back, I'm a Doctor!](/Feats.aspx?ID=7480) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 95

**Archetype** [Lepidstadt Surgeon](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=302)

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42); [Lepidstadt Surgeon Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7472)

**Requirements**
You are holding [healer's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2727), or you are wearing them and have a hand free; a creature with the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition is in your reach.

</column>

---

You have mastered techniques to aid those at the brink of death, even if they use dangerous Stasian technology. Attempt a Medicine check on the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) creature as you deliver it a powerful shock. The DC is usually a [standard-difficulty DC](/Rules.aspx?ID=2627) of a level equal to the target. On a success, the creature’s dying condition is reduced by 1 (or 2 on a critical success); if this reduces the value of the dying condition to 0, the creature regains 2d8+10 Hit Points, its wounded condition doesn’t increase, and it can Stand as a free action. Each creature except you adjacent to the target takes 8d6 electricity damage (basic Reflex save against your class DC or spell DC); instead of attempting this save, a creature can Step as a free action and if they end this movement not adjacent to the target, they take no damage.
name: Stand Back, I'm a Doctor!
requirement_markdown: You are holding [healer's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2727), or you are wearing them and have a hand free; a creature with the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition is in your reach.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Runelord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7482) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Convocation</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Class" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=397" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 116

**Archetype** [Runelord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=303)

**Prerequisites**
[runelord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=303)

</column>

---

Your connection to your sin suffuses all magic you use. When you contemplate or indulge in your sin to Refocus, you can also exchange one spell you have prepared for one of your curriculum or sin spells of the same rank.

<title level="2">Runelord Dedication leads to...</title>
[Embed Aeon Stone](/Feats.aspx?ID=7483), [Rod of Rule](/Feats.aspx?ID=7485), [Sin Counterspell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7488), [Sin Reservoir](/Feats.aspx?ID=7487), [Sinbladed Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7486)
name: Runelord Dedication

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Embed Aeon Stone](/Feats.aspx?ID=7483) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Downtime" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=580" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=695" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 116

**Archetype** [Runelord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=303)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37); [Runelord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7482)

</column>

---

You discover the secrets to embedding _[aeon stones](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3055)_ into your flesh. You spend 1 day attuning to an _aeon stone_ and physically embedding it in your skin. While the stone is embedded this way, you gain the benefits of the _aeon stone_ as if it were orbiting above your head, but it protects the stone from being noticed or stolen as easily. _Aeon stones_ in your flesh must be invested to function, as usual.

 You can also use this activity to safely remove an embedded _aeon stone_ in 1 day. Someone without this feat can attempt to surgically remove it safely by spending 1 day and succeeding at a DC 30 Medicine check, or hastily by simply ripping it from a corpse.

<title level="2">Embed Aeon Stone leads to...</title>
[Aeon Resonance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7484)
name: Embed Aeon Stone

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Aeon Resonance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7484) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 116

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The Aeon Resonance feat acts as if an aeon stone was embedded in a wayfinder, and thus would not allow a second aeon stone to provide its resonance power from being embedded in a wayfinder._

**Archetype** [Runelord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=303)

**Prerequisites**
[Embed Aeon Stone](/Feats.aspx?ID=7483)

</column>

---

You gain the resonant power of one embedded aeon stone as if it were placed in a [wayfinder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3117). While you can embed multiple _[aeon stones](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3055)_ in your flesh, you can gain the resonance power from only one embedded stone at a time, selected each day when you make your daily preparations.

**Special** At 8th level, you can take this feat again. If you do, you gain the resonance powers of up to four invested aeon stones instead of only one.
name: Aeon Resonance

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rod of Rule](/Feats.aspx?ID=7485) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 116

**Archetype** [Runelord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=303)

**Prerequisites**
[Runelord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7482)

</column>

---

Your personal rune is connected more closely to your arcane bond. You gain the critical specialization effects of your arcane bonded weapon. In addition, whenever you critically hit a creature with your arcane bonded weapon, they begin to feel the pull of your sin, imposing a –2 circumstance penalty to saves against any of your curriculum or sin spells until the end of your next turn.

<title level="2">Rod of Rule leads to...</title>
[Orichalcum Bond](/Feats.aspx?ID=7489)
name: Rod of Rule

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sinbladed Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7486) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Spellshape" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=513" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 116

**Archetype** [Runelord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=303)

**Prerequisites**
[Runelord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7482)

</column>

---

You punctuate your spellcasting with a flourish of your weapon, imparting physical force to your magic. If the next spell you cast is a non-cantrip sin or curriculum spell that affects a single target, and you either succeed on your attack roll or the target fails its saving throw against the spell, a wound in the shape of your personal rune appears on the target, dealing additional persistent bleed damage equal to the spell’s rank, in addition to its regular effects.
name: Sinbladed Spell

    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sin Reservoir](/Feats.aspx?ID=7487) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Runelord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7482)

**Requirements**
You are holding a weapon in the [polearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=11) or [spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14) group.

</column>

---

During your daily preparations, you can indulge in your associated sin or meditate on its values (and risks). When you do, you gain one additional spell slot of any spell level up to two levels below the highest-level wizard spell you can cast. You can prepare only one of your curriculum spells in this slot.
name: Sin Reservoir
requirement_markdown: You are holding a weapon in the [polearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=11) or [spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14) group.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sin Counterspell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7488) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 117

**Archetype** [Runelord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=303)

**Prerequisites**
[Runelord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7482); [Counterspell](/Feats.aspx?ID=4994)

</column>

---

Your deep connection to a specific sin allows you to easily negate spells that sin opposes. Instead of being able to counter a foe’s spell with Counterspell only if you have the same spell prepared, you can Counterspell it with any of your curriculum spells if the spell cast would violate your sin’s anathema.
name: Sin Counterspell

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Orichalcum Bond](/Feats.aspx?ID=7489) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 117

**Archetype** [Runelord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=303)

**Prerequisites**
[Rod of Rule](/Feats.aspx?ID=7485)

</column>

---

The ancient Thassilonians associated sins with different skymetals, and you’ve adapted that theory to empower your bonded weapon beyond its normal limits. When your personal rune is placed on a weapon during your daily preparations, choose a weapon property rune of up to your level that is common or you otherwise have access to. That rune is also added to the weapon until your next daily preparations. This rune counts against your weapon’s limit as normal.
name: Orichalcum Bond

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Performer's Treatment](/Feats.aspx?ID=7491) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Convocation</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=615" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=695" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 91

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42); Expert in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45)

</column>

---

With your experience as a performer, you can keep a set of medical tools handy at all times. You can wear a [healer's toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2727) as part of any costume or disguise. Creatures will generally only notice the toolkit it if they use a [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) action and succeed at a Perception check against your Performance DC.

 In addition, you can realize at the last moment when you should just be pretending to avoid doing any lasting harm. When you critically fail a Medicine check to [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396), [Treat Disease](/Actions.aspx?ID=2397), [Treat Poison](/Actions.aspx?ID=2398), or [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399), you can attempt a Performance check against the patient’s Perception DC. On a success, your critical failure at the Medicine check becomes a failure.
name: Performer's Treatment

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Flood Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7492) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Convocation</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=656" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=701" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=732" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 80

</column>

---

The lessons of the flood teach a stance that distributes and circulates a single breath through the body and allows your punches to contain the focused power of a torrent of water. While in this stance, you [hold your breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439) for up to 10 minutes and leave the stance if you stop holding your breath. You do not lose additional air if you attack, are critically hit, or critically fail a save. You still lose all remaining air if you speak, including to Cast a Spell. The only Strikes you can make are flooded river unarmed attacks. These deal 1d8 bludgeoning damage; are in the brawling group; have the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719), and [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) traits; and don’t take the normal penalty for being used underwater. They also gain the forceful trait while you’re underwater.

<title level="2">Flood Stance leads to...</title>
[Culvert's Collapse](/Feats.aspx?ID=7498)
name: Flood Stance

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Reflective Ripple Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7493) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=656" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=701" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=732" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 80

**Requirements**
You’re unarmored.

</column>

---

You enter a stance of fluid grace as small amounts of water flow with your movements and attacks. You can make flowing wave attacks that deal 1d6 bludgeoning damage. They are in the brawling group and have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719), and [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) traits.

 While in Reflective Ripple Stance, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382), and you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Reflex DC to avoid being Disarmed and Tripped.

**Special** This feat gains your choice of either the [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) or [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662) trait, matching your qi spell tradition if possible.

<title level="2">Reflective Ripple Stance leads to...</title>
[Rippling Spin](/Feats.aspx?ID=7499), [Wave Spiral](/Feats.aspx?ID=7504)
name: Reflective Ripple Stance

    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Waterfowl Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7495) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Convocation</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=656" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=701" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=732" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 80

**Prerequisites**
[Monastic Weaponry](/Feats.aspx?ID=5979)

</column>

---

In your hands, swords dance like rainfall over the waves; who can tell where the storm ends and the sea begins? Developed by Swordmaster Ganhil to compete with the House of Untwisting Iron, this stance integrates fluid swordplay into even the simplest movements.

 While in Waterfowl Stance, the dandpatta, scimitar, talwar, and zulfikar gain the monk trait for you. When you [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370) a creature’s space or [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) over a creature while wielding one of these weapons, you can deal that creature 1d6 slashing damage. This damage is increased to 2d6 if the weapon has a _[greater striking rune](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829)_ and 3d6 if it has a _[major striking rune](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829)._

**Special** You gain access to the [dandpatta](/Weapons.aspx?ID=260), [talwar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=266), and [zulfikar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=269)

<title level="2">Waterfowl Stance leads to...</title>
[Snakebird's Shadow](/Feats.aspx?ID=7500)
name: Waterfowl Stance

Ganhil developed this technique through observing the sharp-beaked darters fishing Prada Hanam’s waters, seeking to match their speed and suppleness with the elemental flash of his blade. Each attack counts toward your multiple attack penalty, but you do not increase your penalty until you have made all your attacks.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Class" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=397" />

**Archetype** [Battle Harbinger](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=304)

**Prerequisites**
[battle creed](/Doctrines.aspx?ID=6)

</column>

---

You have trained extensively in combat, battlefield tactics, and stamina, focusing on being an exceptional warrior for your faith in exchange for less time studying the traditional spells and scriptures. You become trained in your choice of [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) or [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34), if you are already trained in both skills, you instead become trained in another skill of your choice. You gain the [Toughness](/Feats.aspx?ID=5227) general feat. If you already have this feat, you gain another general feat of your choice.

<title level="2">Battle Harbinger Dedication leads to...</title>
[Aura Enhancement](/Feats.aspx?ID=7506), [Aura Expertise](/Feats.aspx?ID=7514), [Creed Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=7510), [Exigent Aura](/Feats.aspx?ID=7508), [Harbinger's Armament](/Feats.aspx?ID=7511), [Harbinger's Protection](/Feats.aspx?ID=7509), [Live the Creed](/Feats.aspx?ID=7516), [Tandem Auras](/Feats.aspx?ID=7513), [Tandem Onslaught](/Feats.aspx?ID=7507), [Wither Away](/Feats.aspx?ID=7314)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tandem Onslaught](/Feats.aspx?ID=7507) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Battle Harbinger Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7505)

</column>

---

You have trained your body and mind to work in tandem, and you can combine your combat and spellcasting prowess to better support yourself in battle. The first time each round that you successfully hit and deal damage to an enemy creature with a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) using a weapon or unarmed attack, you can automatically [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) a single battle aura that you currently have active, applying any additional effects that come with Sustaining the spell.

<title level="2">Tandem Onslaught leads to...</title>
[Empowered Onslaught](/Feats.aspx?ID=7512)
name: Tandem Onslaught

[Battle Harbinger Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7505)

</column>

---

You’ve expanded your divine capabilities, granting you magic that better supports your combat focus. You gain two special 2nd-rank creed spell slots, which can be used to prepare [_resist energy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651), [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), [_sure strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709), and [_water breathing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1755) as [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spells. At 10th level, the extra slots increase to 3rd rank and you add [_haste_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553) and [_heroism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555) to the spells you can prepare in your creed spell slots. At 14th level, the extra slots increase to 4th rank and you add [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534) and [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732).
name: Creed Magic

Select one weapon or [_handwraps of mighty blows_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3086) when you make your daily preparations. While in your hands it gains the effect of one [property rune](/Rules.aspx?ID=3168). Choose either [_fearsome_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2837), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [_returning_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2845), [_shifting_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2846), or [_vitalizing_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2852). This rune does not count toward your maximum rune count, and this choice lasts 24 hours or until you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Oath of The Avenger](/Feats.aspx?ID=7517) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />
<trait label="Oath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=119" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 276

**Prerequisites**

</column>

---

You’ve sworn an oath to punish wicked acts you witness. You gain the edict “hunt down those who have harmed innocents or committed heinous atrocities.” When you see a creature harm an ally, an innocent, or a noncombatant, you can invoke your oath against that creature. Like speaking, this doesn’t require an action. Most champions choose to invoke these oaths out loud if they’re able. The invocation lasts indefinitely, though you can choose to end if you are satisfied that the target has reformed, atoned, or been forgiven.

You can use [_lay on hands_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2047) to damage a creature you have invoked your oath against as if it were [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722); in this case, lay on hands deals [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage instead of [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) damage, gains the spirit trait, and loses the vitality trait. This doesn’t prevent you from healing such a creature with _lay on hands_ if you so choose.
name: Oath of The Avenger

</summary>
summary_markdown: You’ve sworn an oath to punish wicked acts you witness.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Oath of The Defender](/Feats.aspx?ID=7518) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />
<trait label="Oath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=119" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 276

</column>

---

During your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), you can swear an oath to defend against a certain kind of enemy during your deeds that day. Choose [aberrations](/Traits.aspx?ID=523), [celestials](/Traits.aspx?ID=551), [dragons](/Traits.aspx?ID=581), [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722). You can’t choose celestials if you’re [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), nor can you choose fiends if you’re [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521). You gain an edict to defend against creatures of that kind.

Allies in your champion’s aura, not including you, get [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 2 to damage dealt by creatures with the chosen trait. If such a creature deals more than one damage type at once, apply this resistance only to the highest amount of damage. The resistance increases to 3 at 7th level, 4 at 12th level, and 5 at 17th level. If a creature with the chosen trait triggers your champion’s reaction and your champion’s reaction grants resistance against the triggering damage, the resistance increases by 5.
name: Oath of The Defender

<traits>

</additional-info>

---

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Oath of The Slayer](/Feats.aspx?ID=7519) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />
<trait label="Oath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=119" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 276

</column>

---

During your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), you can swear an oath to defeat, topple, or destroy a certain kind of enemy during your deeds that day. Choose [aberrations](/Traits.aspx?ID=523), [celestials](/Traits.aspx?ID=551), [dragons](/Traits.aspx?ID=581), [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722). You can’t choose celestials if you’re [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), nor can you choose fiends if you’re [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521). You gain an edict to banish or slay creatures of that kind.

Your [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) and devotion spells that deal damage do an additional 1 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage against a creature with the chosen trait. This damage increases to 2 at 7th level and 3 at 14th level. If a creature with the chosen trait triggers your champion’s reaction, this additional damage doubles until the end of your next turn.
name: Oath of The Slayer

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sense Holiness](/Feats.aspx?ID=7520) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 277

**Prerequisites**

</column>

---

The presence of goodness sickens you with its saccharine self-righteousness. You can sense the presence of creatures with the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait within 120 feet. This functions as a vague sense, similar to humans’ sense of smell. A holy creature using a disguise or otherwise trying to hide its presence attempts a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check against your [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) DC to hide its sanctification from you. If the creature succeeds at its Deception check, it is then temporarily immune to your Sense Holiness for 1 day.
name: Sense Holiness

<traits>
<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 277

**Archetype** [Exorcist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=171) (Level 14)

**Prerequisites**

</column>

---

You sense the truly wicked as a queasy or foreboding feeling. You can sense the presence of creatures with the unholy trait within 120 feet. This functions as a vague sense, similar to humans’ sense of smell. An unholy creature using a disguise or otherwise trying to hide its presence attempts a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check against your [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) DC to hide its sanctification from you. If the creature succeeds at its Deception check, it is then temporarily immune to your Sense Unholiness for 1 day.
name: Sense Unholiness

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Enforce Oath](/Feats.aspx?ID=7522) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 277

**Frequency**
once per hour

**Prerequisites**
Any feat with the [oath](/Traits.aspx?ID=119)  trait

**Requirements**
You can see a creature you’ve sworn an oath against.

</column>

---

You call on your oath to embolden you in combat. Select one creature you can see that you’ve sworn an oath against (per the terms of your oath feat). You gain a +1 status bonus to AC and saves against attacks and effects by the chosen creature. You also gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls against the chosen creature. However, your dedication draws your focus away from all other enemies. You take a –1 status penalty to AC, attack rolls, and saves against all other creatures until you stop Enforcing your Oath.

Enforce Oath lasts indefinitely, but it ends if the enemy is reduced to 0 Hit Points, the enemy offers a legitimate surrender to you or your allies, you fall [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), or the enemy goes [unnoticed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=98) by you for more than 1 minute. You can also [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the effect as a free action.
name: Enforce Oath
requirement_markdown: You can see a creature you’ve sworn an oath against.

    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"

[Pale Horse](/Feats.aspx?ID=7523) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />

**Prerequisites**
[Faithful Steed](/Feats.aspx?ID=5885); 

</column>

---

Your mount becomes a grotesque creature of foreboding when you ride it. Its appearance transforms as flames erupt from its skin, a whorl of void energy surrounds it, or poison leaks from its flesh. When you [Mount](/Actions.aspx?ID=2314) your faithful steed, you can choose [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510). As long as you ride it, your steed gains [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 10 to the chosen damage type, and any creature that touches your steed takes 1d6 damage of the chosen type; this includes hitting the steed with an unarmed attack or with a melee weapon [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) while adjacent to the steed. This damage increases to 2d6 at 16th level and 3d6 at 20th level.
name: Pale Horse

    pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 277

**Prerequisites**

</column>

---

You improve your exalted reaction with the benefit listed for your cause.

**Desecration** While you have the benefit, you also gain a +2 status bonus to saving throws against effects created by enemies in your champion’s aura.

**Grandeur** Any [_revealing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653) created by your Flash of Grandeur lasts for 1 minute.

**Iniquity** The damage dealt to enemies other than the triggering creatures increases by an amount equal to the damage dice of Destructive Vengeance (apply this after you halve the damage).

**Justice** The penalty is –2 instead of –5. 

**Liberation** You and all affected allies can attempt to break free, not just the triggering ally.

**Obedience** The damage dealt to creatures other than the triggering creature who refuse to drop prone increases to half the damage the triggering creature would take (roll the damage only once for all affected creatures). 

**Redemption** The resistance isn’t reduced.
name: Exalted Greatness

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mortal Herald Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7528) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 279

**Archetype** [Mortal Herald](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=305)

**Prerequisites**
Worshiper of a specific deity; Master in [master in Religion or your deity’s divine skill](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)

</column>

---

You become a mortal herald of your [deity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2048), gaining power in return for your service. Others intuitively sense your connection with your deity, which subtly influences the way they act around you. You become bound by your deity’s anathema. Depending on your deity, their sanctification can make you [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521). This gives you the holy or unholy trait, which commits you to one side of a struggle over the souls of the planes and can be referenced in other abilities. If you “can be” holy or unholy according to your deity, you make that choice, and if you “must be” holy or unholy you gain the trait automatically. If you gain the opposing trait in some way, you lose the previous trait until you complete an [atone](/Rituals.aspx?ID=108) ritual.

In addition, once per day, you can cast [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) as a 6th-rank innate divine spell. You can only target yourself with the spell, but you gain the benefits of the 2-action version when you spend 1 action to cast it. In addition, if you would be reduced to 0 Hit Points but not immediately killed, you can cast the spell as a free action before you become unconscious. This spell is automatically heightened to a rank equal to half your level.

**Special** If you have [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83), you instead cast [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552).

<title level="2">Mortal Herald Dedication leads to...</title>
[Declare Anathema](/Feats.aspx?ID=7536), [Deific Font](/Feats.aspx?ID=7543), [Dive of the Divine](/Feats.aspx?ID=7537), [Divine Presence](/Feats.aspx?ID=7544), [Domain Embodiment](/Feats.aspx?ID=7529), [Ease the Burden](/Feats.aspx?ID=7551), [Enter Divine Realm](/Feats.aspx?ID=7552), [Eyes of God](/Feats.aspx?ID=7545), [Fear of God](/Feats.aspx?ID=7530), [Flash of Omipotence](/Feats.aspx?ID=7553), [Flash of Omnipresence](/Feats.aspx?ID=7531), [Flash of Omniscience](/Feats.aspx?ID=7546), [Glory on High](/Feats.aspx?ID=7547), [Guiding Words](/Feats.aspx?ID=7548), [Herald's Weapon](/Feats.aspx?ID=7532), [Heraldic Proclamation](/Feats.aspx?ID=7541), [Invisible Hand](/Feats.aspx?ID=7542), [Marked For Rebuke](/Feats.aspx?ID=7549), [Moment of Apotheosis](/Feats.aspx?ID=7555), [Mortal Ascension](/Feats.aspx?ID=7533), [Shield the Faithful](/Feats.aspx?ID=7534), [Spark of Immortality](/Feats.aspx?ID=7556), [Stand for the Fallen](/Feats.aspx?ID=7550)
name: Mortal Herald Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Worshiper of a specific deity; Master in [master in Religion or your deity’s divine skill](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Domain Embodiment](/Feats.aspx?ID=7529) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 279

**Archetype** [Mortal Herald](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=305)

**Prerequisites**
[Mortal Herald Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7528)

</column>

---

Your deity contains multitudes, and you can express any aspect of them you desire. During your daily preparations, you can gain two initial or advanced domain spells from any of your deity’s domains or alternate domains. You can cast these as innate spells until your next daily preparations. 

Domain spells are a type of focus spell. Casting any of your [focus](/Rules.aspx?ID=285) spells costs you 1 Focus Point. The maximum number of Focus Points in your pool is equal to the number of focus spells you know or 3, whichever is lower. You refill your focus pool during your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), and you can regain 1 Focus Point by spending 10 minutes using the [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=2621) activity to pray to your deity or do service toward their causes. Your domain spells are [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spells. If you were not already, you become trained in spell attacks and spell DCs. Your spellcasting attribute for domain spells is Wisdom.
name: Domain Embodiment

You and those closest to you are protected by your deity’s power. For 1 minute, you and all allies within 10 feet of you gain a +1 item bonus to AC and [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 10 to spirit damage. In addition, when a creature adjacent to you hits you with a melee attack, or when a creature touches you or hits you with an unarmed attack, that creature takes 5 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage.

At 20th level, the spirit resistance increases to 15 and the spirit damage increases to 10.

<title level="2">Shield the Faithful leads to...</title>
[Healing Sanctuary](/Feats.aspx?ID=7540)
name: Shield the Faithful

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 280 **Archetype** [Mortal Herald](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=305) **Prerequisites** [Fear of God](/Feats.aspx?ID=7530) --- With but a mere word from your deity, you exploit a foe’s fear to see their inner being brought low. You chastise the spirit of a foe within 30 feet that has the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition. The target must attempt a Will saving throw against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. The target is then temporarily immune for 1 hour. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes 2 [persistent spirit damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The first time the creature fails their [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to recover from this, the persistent spirit damage doubles.
**Failure** The target takes 4 persistent spirit damage. The first time the creature fails their flat check to recover from this, the persistent spirit damage doubles.
**Critical Failure** The target takes 6 persistent spirit damage. The first time the creature fails their flat check to recover from this, the persistent spirit damage doubles.
name: Chastise

Attempt a [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) check against the target’s Will DC. **Critical Success** Any creature that attempts to target the anathema creature with a beneficial effect must succeed at a DC 5 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) or the action is disrupted. This curse remains until removed by [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) or a similar effect, or an [atone](/Rituals.aspx?ID=108) ritual. In addition, the creature gains [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 10 to [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage for 1 minute.
**Success** As critical success, but the curse lasts for 1 minute.
**Failure** As critical success, but the curse lasts for 1 round and the target doesn’t gain weakness to spirit damage.
**Critical Failure** The target is unaffected.
name: Declare Anathema

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dive of the Divine](/Feats.aspx?ID=7537) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Sanctified" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=519" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 281

**Archetype** [Mortal Herald](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=305)

**Prerequisites**
[Mortal Herald Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7528); [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of at least 20 feet

**Access**
You are at least 20 feet above the ground. 

</column>

---

You dive from the sky, bringing down the might of the divine with you. You [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) straight down, landing safely in the nearest unoccupied space directly below you. When you land, all creatures in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) take 8d10 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC). On a failure, the target is pushed 5 feet (or 10 feet on a critical failure).

At 20th level, the spirit damage increases to 10d10.
name: Dive of the Divine
prerequisite_markdown: [Mortal Herald Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7528); [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of at least 20 feet

The creature you hit in your previous action must attempt a Reflex saving throw against the higher of your class DC or your spell DC. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage equal to half your level.
**Failure** The target takes spirit damage equal to your level and is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
**Critical Failure** The target takes spirit damage equal to twice your level, is knocked prone, and becomes [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 until the beginning of your next turn.

</column>

---

As a mortal herald, you cannot easily be captured, nor can your allies. If you are [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) when you use Flash of Omnipresence, the creature or effect that is imparting that condition on you takes [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage equal to your level. If you teleport adjacent to a grabbed or restrained ally using Flash of Omnipresence, attempt a [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) check against the hard DC for the level of the creature or effect that is imparting that condition. On a success, your ally no longer has that condition.
name: Free from Bonds

Once per day, when you roll [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) for initiative at the start of a combat, you can choose to be [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) for 1 minute. If you spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) while beginning the [Avoid Notice](/Actions.aspx?ID=2622) exploration activity, you are invisible for as long as you continue to Avoid Notice.

once per 10 minutes

**Prerequisites**
[Mortal Herald Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7528); Master in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39)

</column>

---

Your words speak divine truth, guiding blades and arrows to their mark. Choose an ally within 30 feet and attempt a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check. The [DC](/Rules.aspx?ID=2629) is equal to the [hard difficulty](/Rules.aspx?ID=2630) DC of the level of your ally. On a success, the first time the targeted ally makes an attack roll before the end of their next turn, they roll the attack twice and use the better result. Regardless of the result, the target becomes immune to Guiding Words for 10 minutes.
name: Guiding Words

You can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310), [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) instead of Striding if you have the corresponding movement type. You then take the damage that would have brought your ally to 0 Hit Points, though you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to all damage against that damage equal to your level.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

[Mortal Herald Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7528)

</column>

---

You are granted access to your deity’s realm and can travel there quickly. You can cast [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) once per day as an innate [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spell with a casting time of only 1 minute. You act as the planar key for the destination, and you can only travel to your deity’s realm. You and the other targets of the spell always appear in the center of your deity’s realm, and you (but not necessarily your allies) are immune to all the realm’s harmful conditions (such as toxic air or [extreme temperatures](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820)) while there. You can return to your previous location by spending 1 minute picturing your destination; you can bring up to 8 willing creatures back with you in this way, and they need not be the same targets of the previous casting of _interplanar teleport_.
name: Enter Divine Realm

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 283 **Archetype** [Mortal Herald](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=305) **Prerequisites** [Herald's Weapon](/Feats.aspx?ID=7532) --- The power of your blows can send your victim hurling through your deity’s realm for a fragment of a second. You activate Herald’s Weapon, ignoring the frequency, and make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with your transformed weapon or unarmed attack against a creature. If you hit and deal damage, the target must attempt a Will save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. If your Strike was a critical hit, the creature uses the outcome for one degree of success worse than the result of their saving throw. Regardless of the result, the target becomes immune to Herald’s Strike for 10 minutes. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes 3d10 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage and becomes [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Failure** The target takes 6d10 spirit damage and becomes dazzled for 1 round and [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1.
**Critical Failure** The target takes 12d10 spirit damage and becomes [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round and drained 2.
name: Herald's Strike

</title>

<traits>

[Mortal Herald Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7528)

</column>

---

You have become a divine entity, no longer entirely mortal. You no longer age, and you die only when you reach [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) 5 (dying 6 if you have [Diehard](/Feats.aspx?ID=5140)). Upon your death, your body and soul are transported to bask in the presence of your deity. Your god can decide to rebuild your mortal form and return you to the point of your death or some other safe location, or allow you to rest as an honored guest in their realm until you are needed once more. Typically, your god restores you if whatever quest you’re undertaking, such as defeating a malevolent tyrant or returning a stolen artifact, remains incomplete.
name: Spark of Immortality

>
[Palatine Detective Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7557) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Class" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=397" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Archetype** [Palatine Detective](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=306)

**Prerequisites**
[investigator](/Classes.aspx?ID=59)

</column>

---

You have delved into the study of the occult and divine, becoming exceptionally skilled at identifying when such forces are at work. Using this esoteric knowledge and your own ingenuity, you have crafted a small charm that soothes your mind and protects you against harm. This charm usually takes the form of a silver chain, a golden scarab, or a withered hand. While you wear this charm, you gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws against mental effects. In addition, you gain the Mystic Aegis reaction. 

 **Mystic Aegis** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **Trigger** A creature you have successfully [Recalled Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about would damage you; **Requirements** You have a free hand; **Effect** You brandish your charm, channeling and shaping the forces within it to protect you against harm. You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to all damage equal to 2 + half your level rounded up against the triggering effect.

<title level="2">Palatine Detective Dedication leads to...</title>
[Challenge Insight](/Feats.aspx?ID=7562), [Cursebreaker](/Feats.aspx?ID=7563), [Esoteric Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=7558), [Open the Blazing Eye](/Feats.aspx?ID=7565), [Palatine Enchantment](/Feats.aspx?ID=7560), [Palatine Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=7561), [Text Decoder](/Feats.aspx?ID=7559)
name: Palatine Detective Dedication

[Palatine Detective Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7557)

**Trigger**
A creature you can see [Casts a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) that is from the [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) or [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) tradition.

**Requirements**
The triggering caster must be able to hear and understand you.

</column>

---

Your deep grasp of the mystical lets you know exactly what to say to make others doubt how well they understand the source of their own power. Attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) check against the triggering spell. Your counteract rank is half your level rounded up. You use your [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) modifier as your counteract modifier if the triggering spell is from the [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) tradition, or your [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) modifier if the triggering spell is from the [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) tradition. If you successfully counteract the spell, the caster becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 until the end of their next turn.
name: Challenge Insight
requirement_markdown: The triggering caster must be able to hear and understand you.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cursebreaker](/Feats.aspx?ID=7563) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 289

**Archetype** [Palatine Detective](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=306)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Palatine Detective Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7557)

</column>

---

Using various implements and esoteric methods, you can attempt to remove a curse from an unfortunate victim. It takes 10 minutes to perform these rites on a willing creature, who must remain within your reach the entire time. Attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) one [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566) afflicting the creature; your counteract rank is half your level rounded up, and you use either your [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) modifier as your counteract modifier. If the afflicted creature or the individual who placed the curse on the afflicted creature is the target of your active investigation through [Pursue a Lead](/Actions.aspx?ID=2811), you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to this counteract check. If the curse comes from a cursed item or other external source, a success indicates that the target creature can rid itself of the cursed item, but it doesn’t remove the curse from the item.
name: Cursebreaker

>
[Greater Esoteric Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=7564) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

[Esoteric Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=7558)

</column>

---

Your understanding of magic has evolved, and so has your spellcasting. You become an expert in the spell attack modifier and spell DC statistics. Also, choose a tradition from which you can cast innate spells due to the [Esoteric Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=7558) feat. You also gain a 4th-rank spell of that tradition as an innate spell you can cast once a day. At 12th level, you gain a 5th-rank spell, and at 14th level, you gain a 6th-rank spell, both of the same tradition.

**Special** You can take this feat a second time, choosing a different eligible tradition.
name: Greater Esoteric Spellcasting

    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Open the Blazing Eye](/Feats.aspx?ID=7565) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Palatine Detective Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7557)

</column>

---

Thanks to your diligence in the study of divine and occult mysteries, you can focus to temporarily attain a perception beyond the ordinary. A lightless flame appears in the center of your forehead. For 1 minute, you gain the benefits of a [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) spell. In addition, when you attempt a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) during this time against a creature about which you could [Recall Knowledge](/Rules.aspx?ID=2638) using [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46), the attack ignores circumstance penalties to the attack roll and any [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) required due to the target being [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) or [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79).
name: Open the Blazing Eye

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Razmiran Priest Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7566) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 290

**Archetype** [Razmiran Priest](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=307)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37); Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38)

</column>

---

You have earned entry to the priesthood of Razmir, either at the Exalted Lodge or another temple where Razmir’s holy masks are forged. You gain a [Razmiri mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3593) and can [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) a replacement in 4 hours if yours is ever damaged or lost. There is no value in selling your Razmiri mask, and only you gain its benefits. The abilities of your Razmiri mask use the higher of your class DC or spell DC.

You can take the [Cleric Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5066) feat without needing to meet its prerequisites and before you take two other feats from the Razmiran priest archetype, but you must choose [Razmir](/Deities.aspx?ID=357) as your deity. All spells granted by the cleric archetype when gained in this way are [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) spells instead of [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spells, and cleric feats that normally have the [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) trait instead have the [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662) trait. Your key spellcasting attribute for these spells is Charisma, rather than Wisdom.

<title level="2">Razmiran Priest Dedication leads to...</title>
[Living God](/Feats.aspx?ID=7570), [Mask of The 12th Step](/Feats.aspx?ID=7568), [Mask of The 15th Step](/Feats.aspx?ID=7569), [Perfect Truths](/Feats.aspx?ID=7567)
name: Razmiran Priest Dedication

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Perfect Truths](/Feats.aspx?ID=7567) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 290

**Archetype** [Razmiran Priest](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=307)

**Prerequisites**
[Razmiran Priest Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7566)

</column>

---

Your knowledge of Razmir’s faith and doctrines has grown. You excel in debating priests of rival faiths, wielding truths as subtly and deftly as any charlatan might wield a lie. When you use [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389), you can carefully structure your deceits so that each individual statement you make is the truth, from a certain perspective. As long as you are wearing your [Razmiri mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3592), you don’t take a penalty to Deception checks while subject to the [_ring of truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1656) spell or similar effects. Whenever you are subject to _ring of truth_ or a similar effect, you can attempt a Deception check when you first begin speaking to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the spell’s effects, with a counteract rank equal to half your level (rounded up); succeeding at this check doesn’t end the spell or effect, but it does cause it to indicate that you are speaking the truth, even when you are actually lying, as you weave a web of words so delicate that even you can’t be certain where the truth ends and deception begins.
name: Perfect Truths

    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mask of The 12th Step](/Feats.aspx?ID=7568) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 291

**Archetype** [Razmiran Priest](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=307)

**Prerequisites**
[Razmiran Priest Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7566); Expert in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)

</column>

---

You have reached the 12th Step and been instructed in greater secrets of [Razmir’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=357) doctrine. You can [Craft](/Rules.aspx?ID=2634) your [Razmiri mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3593) out of [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) or upgrade your existing mask to a silver one in the same time it takes to Craft one made of iron . A silver Razmiri mask grants a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) or [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390), is a 10th-level item, and gains the following activation in addition to that possessed by the standard mask.

**Activate—Call Upon Razmir’s Mercy** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** Exhorting Razmir to purge impurities from your target, you lay hands on a creature within reach and cast [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) as an [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) spell with a spell rank equal to half your level. Unlike a normal _cleanse affliction_ spell, this doesn’t reduce the stage of the affliction; instead, if the counteract check is successful, the [affliction’s](/Rules.aspx?ID=2389) stage is temporarily reduced by 1 and its effects are suppressed for 24 hours, after which the affliction resumes in full force. If the target would have been required to attempt additional saves against the affliction during the 24 hours it was suppressed, they must attempt all of those saving throws as soon as the 24-hour duration ends. This could mean that the target saves successfully and it is as if the affliction were truly healed, or it could mean that the affliction returns and advances multiple stages all at once.
name: Mask of The 12th Step

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

[Razmiran Priest Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7566); Master in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)

</column>

---

You have ascended to the 15th Step and stand among the greatest of [Razmir’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=357) priests. You can [Craft](/Rules.aspx?ID=2634) your [Razmiri mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3593) out of gold or upgrade your existing mask to a gold one in the same time it takes to Craft one made of iron. A gold Razmiri mask grants a +3 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) or [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390), is a 14th-level item, and gains the following activation in addition to those possessed by the standard and silver masks. 

**Activate—Call Upon Razmir’s Wrath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cry out to Razmir to reveal his fiery wrath, as he did upon the unbelievers at Melcat. You cast [_sunburst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1707) as an 8th-rank [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) spell, but for [undead](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=3) targets the spell has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) trait.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Living God](/Feats.aspx?ID=7570) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 291

**Archetype** [Razmiran Priest](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=307)

**Prerequisites**
[Razmiran Priest Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7566); Legendary in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)

</column>

---

You have reached heights of power equaling [Razmir’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=357) himself. You can [Craft](/Rules.aspx?ID=2634) your [Razmiri mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3593)  out of porcelain laced with precious metals, or upgrade your existing mask to a porcelain one in the same time it takes to Craft one made of iron. A porcelain Razmiri mask grants a +4 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) or [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390), is an 18th-level item, and is an apex item that increases your Charisma modifier by 1 or increases it to +4, whichever would give you a higher score. It also gives you the following activation in addition to those granted by the standard, silver, and gold masks.

**Activate—Power of the Living God** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You demand power from the world, using your mask as a locus to force reality to bend to your will. You cast [_manifestation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1592) as a 10th-rank [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) spell, but no matter what spell you emulate with it, that spell has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) trait.
name: Living God

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rivethun Emissary Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7571) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 292

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) [Rivethun](/Deities.aspx?ID=388) is a philosophy which characters may choose as their religion. Doing so fulfills the “follower of Rivethun” access condition._

**Archetype** [Rivethun Emissary](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=308)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39); Trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)

**Access**
Followers of Rivethun have access to this feat.

</column>

---

You’re a practicing Rivethun emissary, skilled at interacting and bonding with the spirits of living creatures. You become an expert in Diplomacy and Religion. You also gain the Bond with Spirit activity and the [_entreat spirit_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2432) focus spell. If you don’t already have one, you gain a [focus pool](/Rules.aspx?ID=285), which you can regain using the [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=2621) activity to nurture your personal well of spiritual power. Your Rivethun focus spells are [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spells; when you gain this feat, you become trained in the spell attack modifier and spell DC statistics. Your key spellcasting attribute for Rivethun focus spells is Wisdom.

Bond with Spirit ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595)) You can forge a spiritual bond with a creature that is at least friendly toward you or with a terrain that you’re currently in. Forming this bond takes 10 minutes, during which time you must remain adjacent to the creature or within that terrain. If the creature is intelligent, this also requires that creature’s consent, and that intelligent creature can choose to sever this bond with you at any time as a free action, which has the concentrate trait. This bond lasts as long as you are within a mile of the creature or terrain, until you use Bond with Spirit again, or until you make your daily preparations. You must be Bonded with a Spirit to cast the entreat spirit focus spell.

<title level="2">Rivethun Emissary Dedication leads to...</title>
[Consult the Spirits (Rivethun Emissary)](/Feats.aspx?ID=7577), [Domain Spirit](/Feats.aspx?ID=7573), [Emissary Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=7572), [Kaleidoscopic Entreaty](/Feats.aspx?ID=7574), [Manifold Conduit](/Feats.aspx?ID=7578), [Rivethun Devotion](/Feats.aspx?ID=7575)
name: Rivethun Emissary Dedication

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Emissary Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=7572) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 292

**Archetype** [Rivethun Emissary](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=308)

**Prerequisites**
[Rivethun Emissary Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7571)

</column>

---

You forge a mystical bond with a spirit or create a spirit from a fragment of your own soul. You gain a familiar. If you already have a familiar, you gain the [Enhanced Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=4717) feat.

Your familiar gains two additional [familiar abilities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2125) each day, one of which must be used to select the following master ability.

**Synchronize Spirit** You can synchronize your spiritual energy with that of your familiar to temporarily become part spirit yourself. Once per round, you can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) to synchronize your spirit with that of your familiar. You remain synchronized with your spirit until the beginning of your next turn. While synchronized, whenever you [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) or [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) without a duration that deals damage, you deal spirit damage instead of your normal damage.
name: Emissary Familiar

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Domain Spirit](/Feats.aspx?ID=7573) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Exploration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=66" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 292

**Archetype** [Rivethun Emissary](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=308)

**Prerequisites**
[Rivethun Emissary Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7571)

</column>

---

You forge an intense spiritual bond of mutual trust and appreciation with a single spirit that you have already bonded with using Bond with Spirit. Forming this bond takes 24 hours of communion, during which time you must remain within 30 feet of the creature or within that terrain. If the creature is intelligent, this also requires that creature’s consent, and that intelligent creature can choose to sever this bond with you at any time as a free action, which has the concentrate trait. This spirit is your domain spirit. The bond between you and your domain spirit is not weakened by time or distance, but you can only maintain one such bond at a time, regardless of how many bonded spirits you can maintain.

Select one domain related to your domain spirit. You always have the option to select the [healing](/Domains.aspx?ID=78) or [soul](/Domains.aspx?ID=51) domain. If your spirit is a creature, select your domain from the following: [ambition](/Domains.aspx?ID=64), [confidence](/Domains.aspx?ID=67), [family](/Domains.aspx?ID=74), [might](/Domains.aspx?ID=83), [protection](/Domains.aspx?ID=90), or [trickery](/Domains.aspx?ID=94). If your spirit is a terrain, select your domain from the following: [air](/Domains.aspx?ID=65), [cities](/Domains.aspx?ID=66), [darkness](/Domains.aspx?ID=69), [earth](/Domains.aspx?ID=73), [nature](/Domains.aspx?ID=85), [travel](/Domains.aspx?ID=93), [water](/Domains.aspx?ID=98), or [wood](/Domains.aspx?ID=63). If your spirit has a trait that shares a name with a domain, you can add that domain to your list of available options. Your GM can choose to add domains to your list of available options, to represent your domain spirit’s unique interests. The domain you select from among your available options must have a clear connection to your domain spirit. For example, if you bond with a [fire elemental](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=410), you add the [fire](/Domains.aspx?ID=76) domain to the list of options available to you, but if you bond with a deserted tropical island, you can’t select the cities domain, even though it’s among the options usually available to terrain spirits. Your GM is the final arbiter of which domains you can select from. You gain the initial domain spell for that domain. This domain spell is a Rivethun focus spell.

You can choose to bond with a new domain spirit, ending your bond with your current domain spirit and bonding with a different one, to change your selected domain. This process takes 24 hours and follows all the same requirements for forming a bond with your initial domain spirit. During this time of communion, you do not have access to your domain spells granted by your domain spirit. When this bond is formed with your new domain spirit, select one domain related to your new spirit, following the above rules for selecting a domain.

<title level="2">Domain Spirit leads to...</title>
[Advanced Domain Spirit](/Feats.aspx?ID=7576)
name: Domain Spirit

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 293 **Archetype** [Rivethun Emissary](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=308) **Prerequisites** [Rivethun Emissary Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7571) --- If your next action is to cast entreat spirit, you can cause that spirit to unleash a brilliant display of color, light, and sound in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) centered on you. Each creature in the area must attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC or spell DC with the following results. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is dazzled for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round and dazzled for 1 minute.
name: Kaleidoscopic Entreaty

<title

    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rivethun Devotion](/Feats.aspx?ID=7575) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 293

**Archetype** [Rivethun Emissary](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=308)

**Prerequisites**
[Rivethun Emissary Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7571)

**Access**
Followers of Rivethun have access to this feat.

</column>

---

Through study, dedication, and introspection, you've learned to wield magic that affects the spirit. You gain [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) and [_spirit link_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1686) as 2nd-rank [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) innate spells. You can cast each of these spells once per day. If you're a master in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46), _spirit link_ is heightened to 3rd rank. If you're legendary in Religion, _spirit link_ is heightened to 4th rank.

<title level="2">Rivethun Devotion leads to...</title>
[Rivethun Adept](/Feats.aspx?ID=7579)
name: Rivethun Devotion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Consult the Spirits (Rivethun Emissary)](/Feats.aspx?ID=7577) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Exploration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=66" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 293

**Archetype** [Rivethun Emissary](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=308)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Rivethun Emissary Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7571)

</column>

---

You spend 1 hour communing with powerful spirits of the world to gain insight, information, or advice. Select advice or answers.

If you selected advice, choose a particular goal or activity you plan to engage in today, or an event you might expect to happen today. The spirits share with you a cryptic clue or piece of advice that could help with the chosen event, often in the form of a rhyme or omen. 

If you selected answers, ask two clear and concise questions. The spirits answer your questions. If the question is easy for the spirits to answer accurately, they answer clearly. Otherwise, they answer cryptically, often in the form of a rhyme or omen.

Regardless of your choice, the spirits additionally bless you, granting you a +1 status bonus with a skill that the spirits believe will be of use to you today. The GM selects this skill on behalf of the spirits. This bonus lasts until the end of the day.

<title level="2">Consult the Spirits (Rivethun Emissary) leads to...</title>
[Embolded With Glorious Purpose](/Feats.aspx?ID=7580)
name: Consult the Spirits (Rivethun Emissary)

markdown:
**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 294 **Archetype** [Rivethun Emissary](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=308) **Prerequisites** [Consult the Spirits (Rivethun Emissary)](/Feats.aspx?ID=7577) --- Communing with powerful spirits of the world and other powers far greater than yourself fills your heart with purpose and inspires you to strive for greater accomplishments. Whenever you Consult the Spirits, you gain a +1 status bonus to Will saving throws until the next time you make your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575). This bonus increases to +2 against emotion and fear effects. In addition, until the next time you make your daily preparations, you can do each of the following once.
  • Roll a Will save twice and use the better result.
  • Roll an attack roll twice and use the better result.
  • Roll a skill check twice and use the better result; this skill check must be made with the skill that the spirits blessed during Consult the Spirits.
name: Embolded With Glorious Purpose

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rivethun Invoker Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7581) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 294

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) [Rivethun](/Deities.aspx?ID=388) is a philosophy which characters may choose as their religion. Doing so fulfills the “follower of Rivethun” access condition._

**Archetype** [Rivethun Invoker](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=309)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36); Trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)

**Access**
Followers of Rivethun have access to this feat.

</column>

---

You’re a practicing Rivethun invoker, able to enter a trance that connects you physically and mentally to surrounding spirits. You become an expert in Athletics and Religion. You also gain the [Diehard](/Feats.aspx?ID=5140) general feat and the Enter Spirit Trance ability. 

**Enter Spirit Trance** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) You enter a self-imposed trance that helps you push the physical limits of your body. You gain a number of temporary Hit Points equal to your level plus your Constitution modifier. The trance lasts for 1 minute, until you fall [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), or until you [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) it, whichever comes first. While in this trance, you gain a +1 status bonus to Fortitude and Will saving throws and your melee [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) deal 1 additional [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage. Other abilities may require you to be in a spirit trance. When the trace ends, you lose any remaining [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) from Enter Spirit Trance, and you can’t Enter a Spirit Trance for 1 minute.

<title level="2">Rivethun Invoker Dedication leads to...</title>
[Defy Sorrow](/Feats.aspx?ID=7585), [Invoke Defense](/Feats.aspx?ID=7584), [Invoke Movement](/Feats.aspx?ID=7586), [Invoke Offense](/Feats.aspx?ID=7582), [Leverage Anguish](/Feats.aspx?ID=7583), [One with the Spirits](/Feats.aspx?ID=7587)
name: Rivethun Invoker Dedication

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Invoke Offense](/Feats.aspx?ID=7582) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
<trait label="Spirit" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=149" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 294

**Archetype** [Rivethun Invoker](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=309)

**Prerequisites**
[Rivethun Invoker Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7581)

**Requirements**
You’re under the effects of Enter Spirit Trance.

</column>

---

You manifest a physical attack of the spirits all around you, such as the claw of an animal spirit or the whipping vine of a nature spirit. You gain an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) attack that deals 1d8 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage for the duration of your spirit trance. This unarmed attack is in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) weapon group and has the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), and [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) traits. At 5th level, this unarmed attack gains the benefits of a [_striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) rune. At 12th level, this unarmed attack gains the benefits of a _greater striking_ rune. At 20th level, this unarmed attack gains the benefits of a _major striking_ rune.
name: Invoke Offense
requirement_markdown: You’re under the effects of Enter Spirit Trance.

Choose a movement type: [burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310), [climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), or [swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381). You gain a Speed in the chosen movement type equal to your base Speed (or half your Speed if you selected burrow) for the duration of your spirit trance. If you use Invoke Movement again, you can choose a different movement type, but you lose the previous form of movement.

    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[One with the Spirits](/Feats.aspx?ID=7587) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 294

**Archetype** [Rivethun Invoker](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=309)

**Frequency**
once per hour

**Prerequisites**
[Rivethun Invoker Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7581)

**Requirements**
You’re under the effects of Enter Spirit Trance.

</column>

---

The spirits you’ve invoked throughout your life have left behind echoes and spiritual fragments, which you can manifest across your own flesh, temporarily transforming you into something more akin to spirit than mortal. For 1 minute, you gain [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 5 and you become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62). You can’t use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404). Your [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) gain the benefits of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune. Whenever you deal damage with a Strike, you can choose to have that Strike deal [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage, rather than its usual damage type.

If you would be knocked [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) or your [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) value would increase while One with the Spirits is active, the spirits revive you at the beginning of your next turn, restoring you to 30 Hit Points. One with the Spirits then immediately ends.
name: One with the Spirits
requirement_markdown: You’re under the effects of Enter Spirit Trance.
