_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) [Rivethun](/Deities.aspx?ID=388) is a philosophy which characters may choose as their religion. Doing so fulfills the “follower of Rivethun” access condition._

**Archetype** [Rivethun Involutionist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=310)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43); Trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)

**Access**
Followers of Rivethun have access to this feat.

</column>

---

You're a practicing Rivethun involutionist, with a deep well of inner power. You become an expert in Nature and Religion. You cast spells and gain the Cast a Spell activity. You gain a spell repertoire with two common cantrips from the divine spell list or any other divine cantrips you've learned or discovered. You're trained in the spell attack modifier and spell DC statistics. Your key spellcasting attribute for Rivethun involutionist archetype spells is Wisdom.

<title level="2">Rivethun Involutionist Dedication leads to...</title>
[Basic Rivethun Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=7589), [Spirit Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=7590)

You gain the service of a [young animal companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=2114), which has been blessed by the spirits of this world. If you already have an animal companion, your current animal companion becomes blessed by the spirits instead. This animal companion follows all the usual rules for animal companions. You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) to activate your companion’s latent blessing, as long as your animal companion is within 60 feet of you. When you activate this blessing, all damage dealt by your animal companion’s Strikes deal [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage, rather than the usual damage those Strikes would deal. This blessing remains active until the beginning of your next turn.

>
[Splinter Faith](/Feats.aspx?ID=7596) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=811" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=554" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 9

</column>

---

Your faith in your deity is represented in an extremely unusual way that some might call heretical. When you select this feat, you should detail the fundamental tenets of your splinter faith, though it shouldn't affect the deity's edicts or anathema. Choose four domains. These domains must be chosen from among your deity's domains, your deity's alternate domains, and up to one domain that isn't on either list and isn't anathematic to your deity. Any domain spell you cast from a domain that isn't on either of your deity's lists is always heightened to 1 rank lower than usual for a focus spell. For the purpose of abilities that depend on your deity's domains, the four domains you chose are your deity's domains, and any of your deity's domains you didn't choose are now among your deity's alternate domains.

**Special** Unless you take this feat at 1st level, changing the way you relate to your deity requires retraining, as described in the [Changing Faith](/Rules.aspx?ID=3350) section. If you take this feat and previously benefited from any effect that requires a domain your splinter faith doesn't include, such as a domain spell from [Domain Initiate](/Feats.aspx?ID=4644), you lose that effect. 

At the GM's discretion, you can take this feat with other classes or archetypes that gain access to domains through a deity.
.
name: Splinter Faith

**Success** You gain 5 extra feet of movement to your Speeds until the end of your Stride action.
**Failure** You lose 5 feet of movement from your Speeds until the end of your Stride action.
**Critical Failure** Your character softlocks and is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) permanently. Only a [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual or similar magic can reverse this effect.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs=""
>
[Venture-Gossip Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7599) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Foolish Housekeeping and Other Articles](/Sources.aspx?ID=239)

**Archetype** [Venture-Gossip](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=311)

**Prerequisites**
[Pathfinder Agent Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=871); member of the Pathfinder Society

</column>

---

_Nethys Note: This is a part of the April Fool's content from Paizo's blogs._

Look, the motto of the Pathfinder Society is “explore, report, cooperate” and you're just reporting what you hear! Is all of it accurate or true? Maybe not, but hey, you can tell a great story and you know how to keep your readers hooked and eating out of the palm of your hand. Ambrus Valsin of the Grand Lodge may disapprove of your methods, but hey, he's not your real boss. 

 Eando isn't either. 

 We'll go with Zarta. 

 Wait, no, Sheila. Sheila appreciates a good gossip column. You're now from Magnimar. Always have been. 

 You have two identities: your day job as a Pathfinder agent, and your real job, one of the many voices that make up the Venture-Gossip tabloid. If someone attempts to figure out if you're a venture-gossip, they must use a Seek action to attempt a Perception check against one of your influence skills of your choice: Deception, Diplomacy, or Intimidation. Your DC is 20 + your proficiency modifier. 

 If you are discovered to be part of Venture-Gossip, you can spend 1 week of downtime convincing others that you've never even read that tabloid, much less contributed to it! 

 You gain the following action, allowing you to collect more information for your column. 

 **Tell Me More** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Target** one creature that shares a language you speak; **Requirements** You're not in combat; **Effect** Just batting your eyes and giving someone a familiar nudge can get them talking, ready to spill the beans about everything and everyone around them. You roll twice on all rolls to influence the target and use the better result.

**Special** You can select the dedication feat for this archetype even if you haven’t yet gained three feats from the Pathfinder Agent archetype.

<title level="2">Venture-Gossip Dedication leads to...</title>
[A Little Bird Told Me...](/Feats.aspx?ID=7602), [Catch and Kill](/Feats.aspx?ID=7604), [Define "Report"](/Feats.aspx?ID=7603), [Hey! Over Here!](/Feats.aspx?ID=7601), [Muckraking](/Feats.aspx?ID=7605), [Verbose Distraction](/Feats.aspx?ID=7600)
name: Venture-Gossip Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: [Pathfinder Agent Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=871); member of the Pathfinder Society

    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs=""
>
[Hey! Over Here!](/Feats.aspx?ID=7601) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=541" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Foolish Housekeeping and Other Articles](/Sources.aspx?ID=239)

**Archetype** [Venture-Gossip](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=311)

**Prerequisites**
[Venture-Gossip Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7599)

**Trigger**
An ally within 30 feet rolls a saving throw against an auditory effect.

</column>

---

_Nethys Note: This is a part of the April Fool's content from Paizo's blogs._

Hey! Don’t listen to that! That’s bad sounds! Listen to me! Would you like to hear about how the high priest of the Abadaran church in Absalom’s Petals District is having an affair with an Urgathoan cleric? Yeah, I’m thinking about calling the article “Boney Money!” Your targeted ally treats the result of their saving throw as one degree of success better. Once an ally has been targeted by this effect, they’re immune for 24 hours.
name: Hey! Over Here!

**Source** [Foolish Housekeeping and Other Articles](/Sources.aspx?ID=239) **Archetype** [Venture-Gossip](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=311) **Prerequisites** [Venture-Gossip Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7599) --- _Nethys Note: This is a part of the April Fool's content from Paizo's blogs._ You transform into a small bird, such as a finch or a sparrow, allowing you to eavesdrop on others and gain information you wouldn’t otherwise be able to acquire. You become Tiny, gain the animal trait, and you can’t make Strikes. You can Dismiss the effect to return to your normal shape, and you resume your normal shape automatically if you’re reduced to 0 Hit Points.You gain the following statistics and abilities:
  • AC = 20 + your level. Ignore your armor’s check penalty and Speed reduction.
  • Fly speed 25 feet.
  • Weakness 5 to physical damage.
  • Low-light vision and imprecise scent 30 feet.
  • Acrobatics and Stealth modifiers of +10, unless your own is higher; Athletics modifier –4.
name: A Little Bird Told Me...

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=612" />
</traits>

[Venture-Gossip Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7599); Master in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39)

</column>

---

_Nethys Note: This is a part of the April Fool's content from Paizo's blogs._

Look, getting caught in a lie is a normal part of venture-gossip life. Sometimes you have to give a sheepish smile and apologize for what you've done. When you fail a Deception check, reroll with your Diplomacy modifier. On a success, there are no hard feelings and the other person's attitude condition doesn't worsen. On a critical success, you can attempt the Deception again with no consequences.
name: Define "Report"

---

When you've decided to quash a story, no one can escape from you with any incriminating information. Make a melee Strike against the triggering creature. On a hit, the triggering creature is [Grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), and on a critical hit, they're [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1. This Strike doesn't count toward your multiple attack penalty, and your multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Strike.

---

_Nethys Note: This is a part of the April Fool's content from Paizo's blogs._

It seems like your knowledge has come in handy after all and you have your enemy at your feet. Pulling out your fan mail, you read your fans' scathing remarks about your enemy to their face, letting them feel just how much everyone hates them and everything that they've done. They hate the way that they walk, they hate the way that they talk.

 Your enemy takes 10d10 mental damage, with a basic Will save. On a critical failure, they're [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the beginning of your next turn and lose all reactions, except to cry, loudly.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Verduran Shadow Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7606) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #201: Pactbreaker](/Sources.aspx?ID=241) pg. 79

**Archetype** [Verduran Shadow](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=312)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48); Trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49)

</column>

---

Your awareness of primal energies allows you to explore forests with preternatural ease. You become an expert in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49). You can substitute your proficiency in Survival for your proficiency in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) when meeting feat prerequisites and gaining additional benefits from feats for being an expert, master, or legendary in Stealth; however, unless you also fulfill the Stealth prerequisite, you can only use those feats in forest terrain. While in forests, you can use your Survival modifier in place of your Stealth modifier when you [Avoid Notice](/Actions.aspx?ID=2622), [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404), [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405), or would use Stealth to roll initiative.

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat until you’ve gained two other feats from the Verduran Shadow archetype.

<title level="2">Verduran Shadow Dedication leads to...</title>
[Canopy Predator](/Feats.aspx?ID=7607), [Death from Above](/Feats.aspx?ID=7610), [Sense the Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=7611), [Underbrush Trailblazer](/Feats.aspx?ID=7608), [Verduran Ambush](/Feats.aspx?ID=7609)
name: Verduran Shadow Dedication

[Canopy Predator](/Feats.aspx?ID=7607) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

**Prerequisites**
[Verduran Shadow Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7606); Trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36)

</column>

---

You are as at home in the treetops as you are beneath their branches. You gain a [climb Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2349) of 15 feet when moving through trees, vines, and other foliage. Whenever you succeed at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) a tree or an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) on a branch, you get a critical success instead. You aren’t [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) while Climbing or Balancing on a tree.

<title level="2">Canopy Predator leads to...</title>
[Death from Above](/Feats.aspx?ID=7610)

If you have master proficiency in Stealth, you can extend the effect to two allies. If you have legendary proficiency in Stealth, you can extend it to four allies.

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood **Source** [Pathfinder #201: Pactbreaker](/Sources.aspx?ID=241) pg. 80 **Archetype** [Verduran Shadow](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=312) **Prerequisites** [Canopy Predator](/Feats.aspx?ID=7607); [Verduran Shadow Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7606); Expert in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) **Requirements** You are standing, [climbing](/Traits.aspx?ID=347), or [balancing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) on a surface at least 10 feet above your target. --- You leap down on your target, using their body to cushion your fall and your momentum to empower your attack. Attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to Leap to a space adjacent to the target. If you land adjacent to the target, compare the Athletics check result against your target’s Reflex DC. When you leap onto a creature in this way, you do not also deal damage for [falling on a creature](/Rules.aspx?ID=2353). Regardless of your result, the target is temporarily [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to your Death from Above for 1 minute. **Critical Success** The target falls and lands prone, reducing the falling damage you take by an amount equal to twice your level. You then can make a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against the target. The Strike gains the [deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570) weapon property and deals an additional 1d6 bludgeoning damage. This additional damage increases to 2d6 if you are a master in Athletics, and to 3d6 if you are legendary in Athletics.
**Success** The target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the beginning of your next turn, reducing the falling damage you take by an amount equal to your level. You can then make a melee Strike against the target, adding the deadly d8 weapon property to the attack.
**Failure** You can make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against the target.
**Critical Failure** You fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), whether you take falling damage or not.
name: Death from Above
requirement_markdown: You are standing, [climbing](/Traits.aspx?ID=347), or [balancing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) on a surface at least 10 feet above your target.

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

**Trigger**
A creature targets you with an [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540) and you can see the attacker.

</column>

---

You sense how an incoming attack moves through ambient primal energy, predicting how best to dodge. The triggering attack targets your [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) DC instead of your AC. Though this allows you to avoid taking penalties to your AC, it doesn’t remove any conditions or other effects causing such penalties. For example, an enemy with sneak attack would still deal extra damage to you for being [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), even though you wouldn’t take the –2 circumstance penalty when defending against the attack.
name: Sense the Strike

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Ardande" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=498" />
<trait label="Conrasu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=343" />
<trait label="Ghoran" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=352" />
<trait label="Leshy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=639" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 69

**Prerequisites**
[ardande](/Traits.aspx?ID=498), [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) trait

</column>

---

You have specialized glands inside your throat, which produce acidic nectar that you can spit long distances. You gain a nectar ranged [unarmed attack](/Rules.aspx?ID=1584) with a range increment of 20 feet that deals 1d4 acid damage. On a critical hit, the target is additionally [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1. Your nectar doesn’t add [critical specialization](/Rules.aspx?ID=2203) effects. You’re [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to your own nectar.

**Special** This feat gains the trait for your ancestry.

<title level="2">Caustic Nectar leads to...</title>
[Potent Nectar](/Feats.aspx?ID=7621)
name: Caustic Nectar

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Unbowed, Unbroken](/Feats.aspx?ID=7613) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Ardande" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=498" />
<trait label="Lineage" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=317" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 69

</column>

---

Your connection to elemental wood manifests as resilience—towering trees, rough bark, deep roots, and patient determination. You gain the Unwavering Resilience reaction.

**Unwavering Resilience**<actions string="Reaction" />** Trigger** You attempt a saving throw against a [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), or [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect, but you haven’t rolled yet. **Effect**You tap into the power of the Plane of Wood, gaining strength and mental fortitude. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus on the triggering save. If you critically succeed at this saving throw, you become bolstered by your success; blossoms and new growth temporarily sprout all over your body, and you gain a number of temporary Hit Point equal to your level. These temporary Hit Points last for one minute. When there temporary Hit Points are lost, the verdant growth subsides.
name: Unbowed, Unbroken

[ardande](/Traits.aspx?ID=498) or [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668)

**Trigger**
A creature successfully [Shoves](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) or [Trips](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) you, or you fail a saving throw against an effect that would move you or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

</column>

---

You react instinctively, sprouting roots from your legs and plunging them deep into the ground to keep your footing. If a force would move you, you move half that distance, to a minimum of 5 feet. If you would be knocked prone, resolve the effect, then you [Stand](/Actions.aspx?ID=2303).

**Special** This feat gains the trait for your ancestry.
name: Quick Root

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

</column>

---

You grow a simple tool from your own body, coaxing flowers, vines, roots, and wood to grow and sprout from your flesh into the desired shape. You create a one level 0 common simple [tool](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2759) with no intricate parts or written text, such as a [crowbar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2718), [rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2748), or shovel. You can’t replicate a tool kit or weapon with this ability. This item lasts for 1 minute, or until you create another tool with this ability, whichever comes first. When duration runs out, the item crumbles to mulch.

**Special** This feat gains the trait for your ancestry.
name: Grow Tool

Select one of the following benefits when you gain this feat. This choice is permanent and can’t be changed.
  • You gain 2nd-rank [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1514) as a [primal ](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) [innate spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=2232). You can cast this spell twice per day and must target yourself. The spell’s duration is 30 minutes.
  • You gain 4th-rank _enlarge_ as a primal innate spell. You can cast this spell once per day and must target yourself.
**Special** This feat gains the trait for your ancestry.

    level="1"
    right="Feat 17"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Irresistible Bloom](/Feats.aspx?ID=7620) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Ardande" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=498" />
<trait label="Conrasu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=343" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Ghoran" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=352" />
<trait label="Incapacitation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=93" />
<trait label="Leshy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=639" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Olfactory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=246" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 70

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[ardande](/Traits.aspx?ID=498) or [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668)

</column>

---

You bloom, sprouting flowers and releasing a pleasant smell, becoming irresistible. All creatures in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a Will save with a DC equal to your Class DC or Spell DC, whichever is higher. On a failure, a creature becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with you, and must spend at least one of its actions on its next turn moving toward you. On a critical failure, you acting [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80) to that creature allows them to attempt another save, rather than automatically ending the fascination.

These effects last until the beginning of your next turn. You can spend one action at the beginning of your turn to [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the effects for an additional round. You can Sustain it in this way for a maximum of 1 minute.

**Special** This feat gains the trait for your ancestry.
name: Irresistible Bloom

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 73

</column>

---

You adapt a creature to live among the trees, improving its ability to [climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369), and [leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298). Target a willing creature within 30 feet. For 10 minutes, it gains a [climb Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2349) equal to its Speed and a +1 circumstance bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to Balance and Athletics checks to [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378) and [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377). When it Leaps, it increases the distance it can Leap horizontally by 5 feet and vertically by 2 feet. If you use Born to the Trees again, any existing one ends.

---
**Level (6th)** You can target up to 5 willing creatures.
name: Born to the Trees

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[The Thousand Lashes of the Weeping Willow](/Feats.aspx?ID=7625) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Impulse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=502" />
<trait label="Kineticist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=504" />
<trait label="Overflow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=506" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 73

</column>

---

You conjure a massive willow tree, which lashes your foes with a thousand branches. The trunk of the weeping willow tree is 10-feet in diameter and appears in an unoccupied space within 500 feet. The tree whips its branches at all your enemies within a 30-foot-radius [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) centered on the willow’s trunk, dealing 10d8 slashing damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save against your class DC. A creature that fails its save is also overcome with sorrow, becoming [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for as long as the willow remains, as it weeps uncontrollably. The willow doesn’t target you or your allies.

The willow tree remains until the end of your next turn, but you can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) it up to 1 minute. The first time you Sustain the impulse each round, the weeping willow attacks again, dealing 4d8 slashing damage to all enemies in the area with a basic reflex save. If you use this impulse again, the previous one ends.

---
**Level (20th)** The initial damage is 14d8, and the Sustained damage is 5d8.
name: The Thousand Lashes of the Weeping Willow

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />

You merge with the blossoms sprouting from you and disperse, becoming a visible cloud of blossoms and petals. You’re amorphous. You lose any item bonus to AC and all other effects and bonuses from armor, and you use your proficiency modifier for unarmored defense. You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to physical damage equal to half your level and are immune to precision damage. You can’t cast spells, activate items, or use actions that have the attack or [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait. You gain a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of 10 feet. You can remain in this form for up to 1 minute. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this effect.
name: Disperse into Petals

May contain spoilers from Curtain Call **Source** [Pathfinder #204: Stage Fright](/Sources.aspx?ID=245) pg. 81 **Archetype** [Gladiator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=251) (Level 12) **Frequency** once per day **Prerequisites** [Gladiator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6333); Master in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) --- You can project your voice into a high-pitched screech that damages your foes. Your roar can be heard twice as far as normal, and creatures within a 15-foot-[cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) take 6d10 [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage and must attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. If your Gladiator’s Roar triggers [Play to the Crowd](/Feats.aspx?ID=6335), you gain a +2 status bonus to your [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check to do so. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and becomes frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, becomes frightened 3, and is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1.
name: Gladiator's Roar

Your training has focused you for the opera, and it certainly shows. You become master in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45), and at 15th level your skill in Performance increases to Legendary. You also become expert in [Theater Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or become master in Theater Lore if you were already expert. When you’re on a stage, in an arena, or otherwise at the focus of a crowd there to see you perform, your eagerness to begin grants a +3 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls if you roll [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40), or Performance for initiative.

**Source** [Pathfinder #204: Stage Fright](/Sources.aspx?ID=245) pg. 82

**Archetypes** [Celebrity](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=245) (Level 12), [Dandy](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=246) (Level 12)

**Prerequisites**
[Celebrity Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6298) or [Dandy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6302)

</column>

---

Your ear for harmony has enhanced your ability with musical magic. You can use [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) to [Learn a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2366) or [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2365) spells and effects that are musical in nature. In addition, you gain the Pitch-Perfect Projection action.

**Pitch-Perfect Projection** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) By using your perfect pitch, you can amplify your voice to cause magical auditory effects to reach further. If your next action this round is to [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) or use an action that creates an auditory effect with a range, increase that range by 30 feet.
name: Perfect Pitch

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

[Dandy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6302); Master in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38)

</column>

---

You have a knack for knowing just how to manipulate groups by saying exactly what needs to be said. When you Lie in a public area in front of a crowd, you can gauge the reactions of the crowd as you speak to know how your [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) is working, and Lie loses its [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=690) trait. You gain the Reactive Falsehood reaction.

**Reactive Falsehood** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You fail to Lie to a creature in public; Effect As your target sees through your lie, you spin your falsehood in a way that utilizes the crowd’s reaction to bolster believability. Re-roll your [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check to Lie with a +2 status bonus and use this check’s result as the actual result to Lie.
name: Play to the Crowd

You're so fond of using your Acrobatic feats on the stage that you've begun adding your own flair to these dances on the big stage. When you Perform, if you do a performance that is either related to dance, acting or opera, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to the roll. If you're legendary in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34), this increases to a +2 circumstance bonus. You gain the Dancing Dodge reaction. 

**Dancing Dodge** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) **Trigger** You're hit by a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) during the first round of combat; **Effect** You take advantage of the fact that your foe isn't ready to anticipate your dance-like moves in combat. Add a +3 circumstance bonus to your Armor Class against the triggering Strike to determine the Strike's actual result.
name: Virtuosic Dancer

</column>

---

Enough of your life has been spent performing that you’ve mastered the art of the quick change—especially when it comes to armor or invested items. You can remove any armor as a 3-action Interact activity. You can don light armor in 2 rounds, or in 1 minute for medium or heavy armor. You gain the Impromptu Investment activity. 

**Impromptu Investment** (1 minute) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** You can remove [investiture](/Rules.aspx?ID=3163) from an item you currently have invested, and this investiture does not count against your daily limit. You can invest a new item in this item’s place.

**Prerequisites**
[Celebrity Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6298) or [Gladiator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6333)

**Trigger**
You’re reduced to 0 Hit Points

</column>

---

In the world of drama, the finale of any performance doesn’t have to be the end, and for you, this now extends to life itself. When damage reduces you to 0 Hit Points, you can use this reaction to suddenly spring back into action. Instead of gaining the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition and falling [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), you regain a number of Hit Points equal to 10 + your level + your Constitution modifier.
name: It's Not Over

You [Delay](/Actions.aspx?ID=2294).

You are always ready to embrace to a change in tempo and adapt your actions to take advantage of unforeseen developments. When you return to the initiative order after you Delay, you do so in an unpredictable way. All enemies are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to you until the start of your next turn, and you become [Quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for the current round but can only use the extra action to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) or [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306).

You can [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292) an ally’s [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) or [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check with a [Theater Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) check. You gain the Swift Choreography reaction.

**Swift Choreography** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642)) **Trigger** An ally within 30 feet would become [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** You call out a quick reminder to your ally about a special move you’d suggested to them at some point in the past, allowing the ally to avoid the triggering effect completely.
name: Fight Choreography
prerequisite_markdown: [Acrobat Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6240) or [Gladiator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6333); master in Theater Lore

**Frequency**
once per 10 minutes

[Celebrity Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6298) or [Dandy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6302); Legendary in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45)

**Trigger**
A creature you’re aware of critically succeeds on a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against you and would deal damage to you.

</column>

---

You dramatically cry out in poetic verse about the tragedies that led to your doom at this very moment, instilling guilt in your attacker. The Strike deals normal damage rather than critical damage as your foe relents on their attack at the last moment as they’re moved by your words, though other effects that happen on a critical hit still occur. The creature that made the triggering Strike must then succeed on a Will save against your Class DC or be [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round by guilt at its actions against you.
name: Tragic Lament

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Tattooed Historian Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7647) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #207: Resurrection Flood](/Sources.aspx?ID=249) pg. 80

**Archetype** [Tattooed Historian](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=313)

**Prerequisites**
Constitution +1; Trained in [Belkzen Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), Trained in [Orc Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), Trained in [Orc Pantheon Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), or (at the GM's discretion) a related [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill

</column>

---

You become trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45); if you are already trained in both skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You gain access to all uncommon [magical tattoos](/Rules.aspx?ID=1495) with the [orc](/Traits.aspx?ID=666) trait.

You gain a [_storied skin_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3658) for free upon gaining this feat (or another magical tattoo of 2nd level or lower if you already have _storied skin_). You cannot have more than one _storied skin_ tattoo, but the frequency of its Living History ability increases by one use per minute for every three tattooed historian feats you have. For every two tattooed historian feats you have, you can [invest](/Rules.aspx?ID=2619) one magical tattoo that does not count against the maximum number of items you can have invested at one time.

<title level="2">Tattooed Historian Dedication leads to...</title>
[Agent of All Holds](/Feats.aspx?ID=7650), [Infused with Belkzen's Might](/Feats.aspx?ID=7652), [Inked Panoply](/Feats.aspx?ID=7651), [Inscribed with Elders' Deeds](/Feats.aspx?ID=7653), [Wrath of the Hold](/Feats.aspx?ID=7654)
name: Tattooed Historian Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Constitution +1; Trained in [Belkzen Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), Trained in [Orc Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), Trained in [Orc Pantheon Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), or (at the GM's discretion) a related [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 3"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Aurochs-Headed](/Feats.aspx?ID=7648) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #207: Resurrection Flood](/Sources.aspx?ID=249) pg. 80

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) or Trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40)

</column>

---

Your words and body language can subconsciously unsettle orcs, giving you an advantage when convincing them to see things your way. Many with this feat use it to travel freely between holds, warning all but the toughest orcs to waylay weaker prey. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to skill checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) or [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) creatures with the orc trait. Enemy [orcs](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=66) who can see you at the start of combat take a –1 penalty to initiative rolls.
name: Aurochs-Headed

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 5"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Spell Survivor](/Feats.aspx?ID=7649) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Orc" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=124" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #207: Resurrection Flood](/Sources.aspx?ID=249) pg. 80

**Prerequisites**
[Orc Superstition](/Feats.aspx?ID=4516)

</column>

---

Once you’ve resisted an opponent’s magic, anything else they throw your way becomes less effective. When you attempt a saving throw against a spell using Orc Superstition or [Pervasive Superstition](/Feats.aspx?ID=4524) and critically succeed, you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 1 to all damage dealt by that triggering spell’s caster until the end of their next turn. At 9th level and every 4 levels thereafter, the resistance increases by 1.
name: Spell Survivor

    pfs="Limited"
>

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #207: Resurrection Flood](/Sources.aspx?ID=249) pg. 81

**Archetype** [Tattooed Historian](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=313)

**Prerequisites**
[Tattooed Historian Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7647)

**Trigger**
You critically fail a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40), or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check.

</column>

---

Orcs across Belkzen recognize you as an honored lorekeeper. You’re adept at leveraging this status and showing off your tattoos to resolve conflicts, change minds, or distract others from a faux pas. You get a failure on the check, rather than a critical failure. If the triggering check was made against a creature with the [orc](/Traits.aspx?ID=666) trait, you can instead add the [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) trait to this ability and reroll the check, treating any critical failure as a failure.
name: Agent of All Holds

</title>

A creature targets you with an [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), and you can see the attacker.

</column>

---

Your tattoos can briefly animate and extend from your skin, granting you a spectral shieldbearer who protects you from harm. Doing so expends one use of your storied skin, granting you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. In addition, you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), and [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage equal to twice your number of tattooed historian feats against the triggering attack.

>
[Infused with Belkzen's Might](/Feats.aspx?ID=7652) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

</column>

---

Your tattoos immortalize the conquests of orc war leaders, such as Belkzen besieging the dwarven Sky Citadel of Koldukar. You can expend one use of your storied skin’s Living History ability to channel this power into your own weapons. Until the end of your next turn, you deal additional [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage with weapons and unarmed attacks equal to 1 plus half the number of [Tattooed Historian](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=313) feats you have.
name: Infused with Belkzen's Might

[Tattooed Historian Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7647)

</column>

---

Your tattoos commemorate unique exploits that (according to legends) only an [orc](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=66) could accomplish. During your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), you can reconfigure part of your storied skin to depict a specific orc hero, granting you a 1st-level ancestry feat with the [orc](/Traits.aspx?ID=666) trait until you prepare again; this ancestry feat cannot require any physiological feature you lack, as determined by the GM. Since this feat is temporary, you can’t use it as a prerequisite for permanent character options, such as for a feat. At 13th level, you can instead gain a 5th-level ancestry feat with the orc trait.
name: Inscribed with Elders' Deeds

    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Wrath of the Hold](/Feats.aspx?ID=7654) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

[Tattooed Historian Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7647)

</column>

---

Your tattoos carry the strength of an innumerable horde, and you can expend one use of your storied skin’s Living History ability to animate your tattoos as ghostly assailants that swarm your foes. These spirits attack all foes in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386), dealing 4d6 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage. The damage increases by 1d6 at 10th level and every 2 levels thereafter. Each affected creature must attempt a basic Will saving throw against the higher of your class DC or spell DC.
name: Wrath of the Hold

</column>

---

Your conviction against the sins that all demons grow from is so strong that you can force a demon to suffer from its sins when you strike a sound blow against them. In addition to doing the normal damage for your critical hit, the demon also suffers the effects of its special [vulnerability](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312). If the demon has no special vulnerability, it instead takes an additional 1d6 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage. This increases to 2d6 mental damage at 13th level and to 3d6 mental damage at 17th level.
name: Shame the Sin

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Elf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=58" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 80

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) or Expert in [Demon Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41)

</column>

---

The [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387) of Tanglebriar have long haunted your dreams, just as they have long threatened Kyonin. You have not only studied these fiendish foes well, but have trained extensively to battle the hordes of demons that serve [Treerazer](/Deities.aspx?ID=324) and are particularly adept at striking desperate melee blows against them. 

When you make your daily preparations, choose one kind of demon, such as [succubus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2897), [omox](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2898), [shemhazian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2900), or Treerazer. For the remainder of the day, the first time you hit one of those specific demons in a round with a melee Strike, you also deal 1d6 additional [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage. At 13th level, the extra damage increases to 2d6 additional spirit damage, and at 17th level, the extra damage increases to 3d6 spirit damage.

<title level="2">Demon Hunter leads to...</title>
[Demon Slayer](/Feats.aspx?ID=7664)
name: Demon Hunter
prerequisite_markdown: Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) or Expert in [Demon Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41)

Make a [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3) [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against a demon. In addition to the normal effects of the Strike, the target must attempt a Will save against your class DC. **Critical Success** The target avoids any additional effect.
**Success** The target takes a –5 circumstance penalty to all their movement Speeds for 1 round as arcane coils of magic meddle with their mobility.
**Failure** As success, but a –5 foot circumstance penalty for 1 minute. While the target is affected by this, Anchoring Arrow attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any teleportation effect that would move the target, or any effect that would transport the target to a different plane. The arrow has a counteract rank of 7th and a +20 modifier to the roll.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except a –10 foot circumstance penalty, and the duration is 10 minutes.
name: Anchoring Arrow

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>

<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 81

**Frequency**
once per day

</column>

---

You can undo the effect of demonic corruption in plants. By spending 10 minutes in contact with a tree that’s been corrupted by demonic presence, you can restore the tree to its original health. This does not prevent that tree from becoming corrupted again in the future. Alternatively, you can spend 10 minutes in contact with a creature with the [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) trait. At the end of this time, you restore 7d8+56 Hit Points to that creature and heal half that amount of your own Hit Points. Additionally, you can attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any one [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566) or [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) afflicting that plant creature as if you had cast [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) heightened to 7th rank. Your counteract rank for this is 7, and your counteract check is equal to your Will save or your spell attack modifier, whichever is higher.
name: Treehealer

May contain spoilers from Spore War **Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 81 **Frequency** once per day **Prerequisites** [Demon Hunter](/Feats.aspx?ID=7660) --- You can deliver a melee blow to a demon that creates an explosion of holy light to try to finish the demon off. In addition to the normal damage from your melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), you also inflict 10d6 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage to the demon. The [demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=573) can resist this additional damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save against your class DC. If the demon is slain by this strike, it explodes in a blast of holy light that fills a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). All creatures in this area that have the demon trait take 10d6 spirit damage and must attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC. **Critical Success** The demon takes no damage.
**Success** The demon takes half damage.
**Failure** The demon takes full damage and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The demon takes full damage and is permanently [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59).
name: Demon Slayer

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eagle Knight Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7666) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 50

**Archetype** [Eagle Knight](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=314)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39); Trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)

**Access**
You’re invited by a current member of the Eagle Knights or the People’s Council.

</column>

---

Eagle Knights are the marshals and envoys of Andoran and are committed to keeping the peace. On the first round of combat, if you roll Diplomacy for initiative, creatures that haven’t acted are off-guard to you. You gain the [Additional Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=5114) skill feat for [Politics Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41). If you were already trained in Politics Lore, you also become trained in a Lore skill of your choice. You can use Politics Lore to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on or make a [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) of government officials (or similar figures) or to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391) about them. If you critically succeed at a check to Gather Information with Politics Lore, you know the information without having to spend any time gathering it.

<title level="2">Eagle Knight Dedication leads to...</title>
[Aura of Confidence](/Feats.aspx?ID=7673), [Bolster Ally](/Feats.aspx?ID=7671), [Commitment to Equality](/Feats.aspx?ID=7667), [Commitment to Justice](/Feats.aspx?ID=7669), [Commitment to Liberty](/Feats.aspx?ID=7670), [Interpose](/Feats.aspx?ID=7668), [Reactive Striker](/Feats.aspx?ID=5084), [Stir Allies](/Feats.aspx?ID=7672), [Talmandor's Shout](/Feats.aspx?ID=7674)
name: Eagle Knight Dedication

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 50 **Archetype** [Eagle Knight](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=314) **Prerequisites** [Eagle Knight Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7666); Expert in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) --- You help an ally shake off any impediment that would give an enemy an unfair advantage. You rally a creature within 30 feet in an attempt to reduce its [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition. If a creature has multiple conditions from this list, choose one. When you’re a master in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39), add [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) to the list of conditions. When you’re legendary in Diplomacy, add [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) to the list of conditions; if the stunned condition has a duration instead of a value, you can’t use Commitment to Equality to reduce it. Attempt a Diplomacy check against the saving throw DC of the effect that caused the condition; if there was no saving throw DC, use the hard DC for the level of the creature, hazard, or item that caused the effect. You can’t treat a condition that came from an artifact or effect above 20th level unless you’re legendary in Diplomacy; even if you can, the [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC increases by 10. You can’t treat a condition that is part of a [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566) or [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) or is a natural state of the target. Once you attempt to treat a target’s condition, that target is immune to further attempts for 1 hour, regardless of the result. **Critical Success** Reduce the condition’s value by 2.
**Success** Reduce the condition’s value by 1.
**Critical Failure** Increase the condition’s value by 1. Commitment to Equality leads to... [Even the Odds](/Feats.aspx?ID=7675)
name: Commitment to Equality

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Talmandor's Shout](/Feats.aspx?ID=7674) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 51

**Archetype** [Eagle Knight](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=314)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Eagle Knight Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7666); Master in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40)

**Requirements**
You witnessed a creature deal damage to an ally within 30 feet since your last turn.

</column>

---

After seeing an enemy harm one of your allies, you deliver a righteous shout in the name of the celestial Talmandor. Attempt an Intimidation check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395), comparing the result to the Will DC of each enemy within a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387); this Demoralize attempt doesn’t take any penalty for not sharing a language. It’s possible to get a different degree of success for each target.
name: Talmandor's Shout
requirement_markdown: You witnessed a creature deal damage to an ally within 30 feet since your last turn.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Prophet of Kalistrade Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7680) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 74

**Archetype** [Prophet of Kalistrade](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=315)

**Prerequisites**
trained in [Mercantile Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41); Trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47); Charisma +2

**Access**
You’re from Druma.

</column>

---

After induction into the ranks of the Kalistocrats, you learned the skills necessary to succeed in business and rise above others by following a set of detailed strictures. Unless you take measures to hide your affiliation, anyone who has heard of the [Prophets of Kalistrade](/Deities.aspx?ID=23) will know you’re one of them when they see you. You become an expert in Society and can use Society instead of [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on merchants and traders. 

Additionally, if you don’t already cast spells from spell slots, you learn to cast [spontaneous spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=2224) and gain the [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) activity. You gain a spell repertoire with two common [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) cantrips, plus either [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639) or [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646). You’re trained in the spell attack modifier and spell DC statistics. Your key spellcasting attribute for these spells is Charisma, and they’re occult spells.

<title level="2">Prophet of Kalistrade Dedication leads to...</title>
[Basic Prophet Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=7681), [Fortune's Favor](/Feats.aspx?ID=7683), [Master Merchant](/Feats.aspx?ID=7687), [Prophet's Lockbox](/Feats.aspx?ID=7685), [Smile at Failure](/Feats.aspx?ID=7682)
name: Prophet of Kalistrade Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: trained in [Mercantile Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41); Trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47); Charisma +2
